perturbation.original_text: 1.3 "Bromelain SP" means material derived from pineapple stems, [having the specification as presented in exhibit 1.13] presently manufactured by CBC at the Facility by a special process and used as a raw material in the production of the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 "Bromelain SP" means material derived from pineapple stems, [having the specification as presented in exhibit 1.13] and may or may not be manufactured by CBC, at any facility, using any process, and may or may not be used as a raw material in the production of the Product.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Bromelain SP' strictly defines it as material manufactured by CBC at a specific facility using a special process. The modified definition introduces uncertainty by stating it 'may or may not' be manufactured by CBC, at any facility using any process, which contradicts the initial specific definition and could affect the quality control and consistency requirements outlined later in the agreement. This change undermines the reliance on CBC as the sole manufacturer with a specified process, as defined in Section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Without derogating from CBC's representations and warranties herein, CBC and MediWound shall work together in order to enable the CBC facility to accomplish all required standards, related to the manufacturing, packaging and delivering of Bromelain SP in accordance with the Specifications, GACP (Good Agricultural and Collection Practice) and cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) standards, ISO 22000 and all other applicable laws and regulations. For such purpose, and without derogating from other terms herein, CBC shall permit MediWound, and/or a consultant on MediWound's behalf, to access and inspect the CBC facility and advise MediWound and/or CBC on such actions to be taken for accomplishing such compliance. Such mutual regulatory preparations shall begin no later than the finalization of MediWound's current phase III clinical trial. CBC warrants and represents that all Bromelain SP shall be manufactured and supplied in compliance with the Specifications, quality control methods and test methods, all applicable SOP's and all applicable laws, and in accordance with GACP, cGMP, including the relevant guidelines, policies, codes, requirements, regulations, approvals and/or standards from time to time promulgated or issued by any relevant governmental and/or regulatory authority which relate to the manufacture of the Bromelain SP to be used for the production of a pharmaceutical agent as the Product. CBC warrants further that CBC has, and will for the duration of this Agreement retain, all applicable regulatory approvals required for the carrying out of its obligations hereunder, including without limitation the manufacturing, packaging and supply of the Bromelain SP.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty regarding the source and manufacturing process of 'Bromelain SP'. Specifically, the original definition mandated that it be manufactured by CBC at a specified facility using a special process. The modified text states it 'may or may not' be manufactured by CBC, at any facility using any process. This directly contradicts section 5.1, which places explicit reliance on CBC to accomplish required standards related to manufacturing, packaging, and delivering of Bromelain SP in accordance with specified standards and gives MediWound the right to inspect CBC's facility. By removing the certainty that CBC is the sole manufacturer with a specific process, the modified text undermines the purpose and enforceability of section 5.1, which focuses on CBC's facility and its compliance with standards.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2 MediWound shall furnish CBC with a non-binding forecast of its anticipated annual requirements of Bromeline SP by no later than November 1 of each year ("Annual Forecast"), for the next calendar year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the first forecast for the calendar year that commenced on January 1, 2001, was furnished by MediWound to CBC by March 15, 2001. MediWound undertakes to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2 MediWound shall furnish CBC with a non-binding forecast of its anticipated annual requirements of Bromeline SP by no later than November 1 of each year ("Annual Forecast"), for the next calendar year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the first forecast for the calendar year that commenced on January 1, 2001, was furnished by MediWound to CBC by March 15, 2001. MediWound may, or may not undertake to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that MediWound 'undertakes to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year'. The altered text changes this to 'MediWound may, or may not undertake to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year', introducing uncertainty. This contradicts CBC's reliance on a minimum order quantity for maintaining manufacturing and supply capacity of Bromelain SP. The in-text contradiction affects CBC's planning and ability to meet MediWound's demands.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2 MediWound shall furnish CBC with a non-binding forecast of its anticipated annual requirements of Bromeline SP by no later than November 1 of each year ("Annual Forecast"), for the next calendar year. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the first forecast for the calendar year that commenced on January 1, 2001, was furnished by MediWound to CBC by March 15, 2001. MediWound may, or may not undertake to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year. CBC shall maintain, at all times, manufacture and supply capacity of at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast and shall maintain, in coordination with MediWound, inventory of Bromelain SP at its premises of (i) at least [***]% of the applicable Annual Forecast; and (ii) all Bromelain SP components and materials ("the BSP Components and Materials") needed for the manufacture and supply of the Bromelain SP such that CBC can guarantee continuous supply of the Bromelain SP in accordance with MediWound's complete Annual Forecasts. In addition, the inventory of the BSP Components and Materials shall not be less than needed to manufacture [***] months stock of Bromelain SP (compared to the open purchase orders and the applicable Annual Forecast) or longer (respectively) for BSP Components and Materials having a lead time of more than [***] months. CBC shall provide MediWound with quarterly inventory and production reports for Bromelain SP and BSP Components and Materials. Purchase orders issued by MediWound to CBC for quantities within the [***]% of the Annual Forecast shall be binding upon CBC and shall be deemed accepted upon delivery of the purchase order to CBC. Such purchase orders shall be supplied on the date specified in the applicable purchase order provided that the lead time in any purchase order shall be at least [***] days as of the purchase order's date. Purchase orders issued by MediWound to CBC during a certain year for quantities exceeding [***]% of the applicable Annual Forecast shall be binding upon CBC, except that with respect to any amounts exceeding [***]% of the applicable Annual Forecast, CBC's obligation to provide such exceeding quantities shall be based on best efforts and CBC shall have an extended lead time for delivery as shall be agreed upon by the parties on a case by case basis. CBC shall confirm in writing, within 5 days of its acceptance of such exceeding purchase order, and shall state the anticipated delivery date for the exceeding amounts. Without derogating from CBC's obligations under this Agreement, in the event that CBC is unable to supply all the Bromelain SP covered under any purchase order on the dates specified in the applicable supply plans, CBC shall promptly notify MediWound in writing in a separate notice to MediWound of such delay or noncompliance. In such event, and without prejudice to any other remedies available to MediWound, CBC shall use its best efforts to fully comply with the purchase order as soon as possible.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that MediWound 'undertakes to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year', making it a firm commitment. The changed text alters this commitment to 'MediWound may, or may not undertake to order at least [***]% of the Annual Forecast per each year', introducing uncertainty and removing the firm commitment. This directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text for CBC to maintain specific manufacturing and supply capacities, as well as inventory levels, based on the Annual Forecast, because CBC can no longer rely on a minimum order quantity. The change significantly affects CBC's planning and its ability to guarantee continuous supply as the initial, more binding, commitment suggested.

perturbation.original_text: 10.2 CBC may terminate this Agreement by no less than 24 (twenty four) months notice given in writing by CBC to MediWound, or such greater period as may be reasonable for MediWound to establish an alternative source of manufacture of Bromelain SP and/or to acquire sufficient inventory of Bromelain SP for a 24 (twenty four) months period.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.2 CBC may terminate this Agreement by no less than 24 (twenty four) months notice given in writing by CBC to MediWound, or such greater period as CBC deems is reasonable for MediWound to establish an alternative source of manufacture of Bromelain SP and/or to acquire sufficient inventory of Bromelain SP for a 24 (twenty four) months period.
perturbation.explanation: The original text says the termination period can be extended to 'such greater period as *may be reasonable for MediWound* to establish an alternative source of manufacture'. This is now changed to 'such greater period as *CBC deems* is reasonable for MediWound to establish an alternative source of manufacture'. This creates a direct contradiction in Section 10 regarding who determines the extension period, making it unclear and subject to dispute upon agreement termination. It is contradicted as it allows CBC to have unilateral control over determining a termination deadline without considering whether it's reasonable from MediWound's perspective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 CBC may terminate this Agreement by no less than 24 (twenty four) months notice given in writing by CBC to MediWound, or such greater period as CBC deems is reasonable for MediWound to establish an alternative source of manufacture of Bromelain SP and/or to acquire sufficient inventory of Bromelain SP for a 24 (twenty four) months period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 10.2 to give CBC the authority to determine what constitutes a reasonable period for MediWound to establish an alternative source of manufacture, instead of allowing MediWound to determine what is reasonable for them. The contradicted text shows the same changed text. This creates a contradiction because it changes who has the power to determine the length of the extension period, creating a conflict on who decides if the period is reasonable or not. This contradicts the original agreement and makes enforcement unclear, as it gives CBC unilateral control. The change directly introduces an inconsistency in the application of the termination clause, leading to a strong in-text contradiction, adhering to the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
